title: Ervatamia divaricata
taxonomy: Ervatamia divaricata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It belongs to the genus Ervatamia, which consists of several species of woody vines. Ervatamia divaricata is a native plant found in the tropical regions of Asia, specifically in countries such as China, India, and Southeast Asia. The plant is also known as the 'divaricata' or 'twining milkweed' due to its distinctive twining habit and milky sap. Ervatamia divaricata is a member of the Apocynaceae family, which includes other plants like periwinkles and oleander.
uses: Ervatamia divaricata is a plant species traditionally used in various folk medicines, particularly in Southeast Asia. It is reported to have antimalarial, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant's extracts have been studied for their potential in treating various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. However, further research is needed to fully understand its medicinal effects and safety. Its traditional uses may provide a basis for the development of new pharmaceuticals.

intro: Ervatamia divaricata, commonly known as the "divaricata vine", is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, thanks to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Ervatamia divaricata include alkaloids, such as evratamine, ervatamine, and ervatamine A, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Ervatamia divaricata is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, and is also used in the manufacture of capsules, tablets, and other pharmaceutical products.
